spec.overview: This program processes account records by reading from an account file, displaying account information, populating and writing to various output files (account, array, and variable records), and managing file operations. It also includes error handling for program termination.
responsibilities: Read and process account records from an input file.
Populate and write records to multiple output files.
Handle file opening and closing operations.
Abend the program in case of errors.

paragraphs.name: 1000-ACCTFILE-GET-NEXT
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph is responsible for reading the next record from the account file.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement logic to read the next record from the input account file.

paragraphs.name: 1100-DISPLAY-ACCT-RECORD
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph displays the details of an account record.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement logic to format and display account record fields.

paragraphs.name: 1300-POPUL-ACCT-RECORD
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph populates fields for an account record and calls a program to format date fields.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Populate an account record structure and call a date formatting utility.

paragraphs.name: 1350-WRITE-ACCT-RECORD
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph writes the populated account record to an output file.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement logic to write the formatted account record to an output file.

paragraphs.name: 1400-POPUL-ARRAY-RECORD
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph populates fields for an array record.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Populate fields for an array record structure.

paragraphs.name: 1450-WRITE-ARRY-RECORD
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph writes the populated array record to an output file.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement logic to write the populated array record to an output file.

paragraphs.name: 1500-POPUL-VBRC-RECORD
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph populates fields for a variable record.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Populate fields for a variable record structure.

paragraphs.name: 1550-WRITE-VB1-RECORD
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph writes the first part of the variable record (VB1) to an output file.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement logic to write the first part of the variable record (VB1) to an output file.

paragraphs.name: 1575-WRITE-VB2-RECORD
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph writes the second part of the variable record (VB2) to an output file.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement logic to write the second part of the variable record (VB2) to an output file.

paragraphs.name: 0000-ACCTFILE-OPEN
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph opens the account input file for processing.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement logic to open the input account file.

paragraphs.name: 2000-OUTFILE-OPEN
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph opens the main output file.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement logic to open the primary output file.

paragraphs.name: 3000-ARRFILE-OPEN
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph opens the file for array records.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement logic to open the array record output file.

paragraphs.name: 4000-VBRFILE-OPEN
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph opens the file for variable records.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement logic to open the variable record output file.

paragraphs.name: 9000-ACCTFILE-CLOSE
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph closes the account input file.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement logic to close the input account file.

paragraphs.name: 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph is responsible for abnormal termination of the program.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement program abend logic, potentially including calling a system abend utility.

paragraphs.name: 9910-DISPLAY-IO-STATUS
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph displays the I/O status of operations.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement logic to display I/O status messages.

migration_risks: The specific file I/O operations and dataset names are not fully detailed in the provided artifacts, which could lead to incorrect file handling in the Java implementation.
The exact nature of the 'COBDATFT' program's functionality, which is called from '1300-POPUL-ACCT-RECORD', is not specified and needs to be understood for accurate Java translation.
The absence of explicit file I/O statements in the provided artifact details for paragraphs might hide complex I/O logic that needs to be inferred or clarified.
Error handling logic within the file open/close paragraphs (e.g., '0000-ACCTFILE-OPEN') is not explicitly detailed, posing a risk if not fully captured.
